full_prompt: Design the centrifugal compressor impeller of a micro gas turbine for a university capstone micro-gas-turbine benchmark. The turbine is a JetCat-class single-shaft unit with peak rotor speed 120,000 rpm (2000 rev/s, omega = 12566 rad/s). Compressor stage: single-stage radial centrifugal impeller, tip diameter 80 mm, hub diameter 25 mm, 12 primary + 12 splitter blades. Material is Al 2618-T61 for the impeller hub and blades (T = up to 180 C in the compressor exit; Al 2618 retains good strength at this temperature; yield 360 MPa at room temp, 280 MPa at 180 C). Under steady operation, the dominant load is centrifugal body force: at 120,000 rpm, tip centrifugal acceleration is omega^2 * r_tip = 12566^2 * 0.04 = 6.31e6 m/s^2 = 6.43e5 g. Blade root stress at hub radius 0.0125 m is (1/2) * rho * omega^2 * (r_tip^2 - r_hub^2) * (cross-section-ratio) for a uniform blade, approximately rho * omega^2 * r_tip^2 / 2 = 2780 * 6.31e6 = 1.75e10 Pa = 175 MPa (spin-alone estimate, discounting tip relief). Pass/fail: (1) maximum centrifugal stress in the impeller at 120,000 rpm and 180 C must not exceed 0.67 x temperature-derated yield = 0.67 * 280 = 188 MPa, (2) maximum blade-tip displacement (elastic growth) must be less than 0.15 mm to preserve 0.3 mm tip clearance to the shroud, (3) first modal frequency of a blade must not coincide within +/- 10% of the per-rev or 2-per-rev excitation at 2000 Hz (blade) or 4000 Hz, (4) at 20% overspeed (144,000 rpm proof test for 30 s), the impeller must not plastically yield. Primary verification is structural FEA (rotating-reference-frame centrifugal body load) plus modal analysis (Campbell diagram check). Vibration modal is a primary gate. Thermal is relevant (blade temperature 180 C degrades yield) and must be baked in as a derated material property; full thermal FEA (conjugate heat transfer) is NOT required. Fluid (aerodynamic pressure loads are small relative to centrifugal) is NOT required. Radiation is NOT applicable. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the impeller, a mass budget, and a report listing impeller mass in g, max centrifugal stress at 120 krpm and 180 C in MPa, blade tip elastic growth in mm, first blade mode in Hz, and stress at 144 krpm overspeed.
short_prompt:
Design the single-stage centrifugal compressor impeller of a JetCat-class micro gas turbine for a university capstone benchmark, with 12 primary + 12 splitter blades integral to a hub-and-shroud one-piece casting running on a keyed shaft through a hub bore. The impeller must sit within a shroud with 0.3 mm tip clearance and keep its first blade modes clear of 1P and 2P excitation at design speed (Campbell-diagram separation).

Envelope: tip diameter 80 mm, hub diameter 25 mm, axial depth on the order of the tip diameter. Material is Al 2618-T61 with the yield allowable derated for 180 C compressor-exit metal temperature.

Load cases.
- LC1 design spin: 120,000 rpm, metal at 180 C.
- LC2 overspeed proof: 144,000 rpm (20% overspeed) for 30 s at room temperature, no plastic yield.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Al 2618 yield at 180 C is a datasheet lookup applied to FEA allowable.

prompt.title: Micro gas turbine compressor impeller
prompt.brief: Design an Al 2618-T61 centrifugal impeller (80 mm tip, 120 krpm, 180 C) with 12+12 blades surviving 20% overspeed.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 0.67 x 280 MPa (Al 2618 at 180 C) yield FoS 1.5
preserve 0.3 mm tip clearance with 0.15 mm margin

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: solid TET10 with rotational body load

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Thermal derating applied via yield lookup; full CHT not in gate.
